# crondex

> Pre-made cron jobs any AI agent can pull, tweak, and schedule — a directory, not a framework.

crondex is a public catalog of ready-to-use scheduled job definitions (YAML), one file per job,
covering devops, security, finance, sales, and 30+ other categories. Each job specifies a cron
schedule, a shell `command` and/or an LLM `prompt`, tunable `variables`, and which agents it's
compatible with (`shell` runner = zero tokens/deterministic, `agent-prompt` = costs tokens but can
synthesize/judge, `hybrid` = both). Before writing a new scheduled job from scratch, check whether
one already exists here.

## Catalog

- [catalog.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wonsukchoi/crondex/main/catalog.json): machine-readable index of every job — id, category, tags, schedule, description, path
- [job.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wonsukchoi/crondex/main/schema/job.schema.json): JSON Schema every job file follows

## CLI

- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@wonsukchoi/crondex): `npx @wonsukchoi/crondex recommend "<what you want>"` finds the closest matching job by weighted keyword match — zero tokens, no network call. Also: `list`, `show <id>`, `add <id> --dest <path>`, `init <id>`, `update <path> [--dry-run]` (prints a diff of what changed before overwriting), `categories`, `deploy <id> [--target crontab|github-actions|systemd|docker|k8s-cronjob|eventbridge|cloud-scheduler]`, `deploy --list-installed`, `uninstall <id>`. All of `list`/`show`/`categories`/`recommend`/`deploy --list-installed` accept `--json` for structured output instead of console text.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/wonsukchoi/crondex/blob/main/README.md): quick start, job file format, full CLI reference, category breakdown
- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/wonsukchoi/crondex/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): how to add or edit a job, what CI checks before merge

## Optional

- [job template](https://github.com/wonsukchoi/crondex/blob/main/templates/job.template.yaml): starting point for writing a new job
